codex_response:
Implemented the ARC2 package for `7d18a6fb` in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_7d18a6fb/__init__.py#L1), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_7d18a6fb/verifier.py#L4), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_7d18a6fb/generator.py#L60). The rule is: find the 7x7 `1` block, read the four inner-corner marker colors, extract the largest diagonal-connected motif of each matching color elsewhere, and place those motifs into the corresponding 3x3 output quadrants with the center row and column left `0`. The Opus summary matched the official examples, so I used it; no `notes.md` or new shared primitive was needed.

Verification passed end to end: `4/4` official examples and `1000/1000` generated examples. The generator keeps the `1` block in a random corner, samples 3x3 motifs from the observed official family, and optionally adds one unused-color distractor, which looked on-distribution in the preview sanity check. Artifacts are at [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/7d18a6fb/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/7d18a6fb/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/7d18a6fb/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/7d18a6fb/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/7d18a6fb/previews/preview_03.png).
